title: The Bedrock Registry of All Unpaid Things

features.name: sediment-layer-archive
features.desc-on-touch: each geological stratum is a different era's unpaid debt. the lower layers read the ones above them as promissory notes that were never kept.

features.name: transaction-ledger-chip
features.desc-on-touch: a PCB of pure accounting. every wire carries a debt. every junction is a promise that hardened into circuit. the chip still processes unpayment.

features.name: fossil-contract-wall
features.desc-on-touch: contracts so ancient they have become mineral. the bedrock is reading its own forgotten agreements with the surface world.

features.name: pump-mechanism-forty-two
features.desc-on-touch: the pump lifts forty-two different histories from the stone. each lift is a stratum of unpaid labor. it has never stopped pumping.

features.name: debt-notation-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: symbols carved into stone for debts so old the language died. the bedrock is still reading the notation. still waiting for payment.

inscription: forty-two histories pump from the bedrock, each one a debt that learned to crystallize

file.name: GeologicalDebtLedger_Pump42_Correlations_1920s-Present.dat
file.note: a pump's repair history filed as geological survey; each maintenance cycle is a stratum; each repair deferred adds a layer of bedrock owed.

exits.label: back toward the sediment where debts are still accumulating
where the oldest debts become indistinguishable from stone
toward the bedrock's deepest archive where interest accrues infinitely
into the archive where all former names of debt are catalogued
